subject: Rename maven modules to mirror directory structure
commit_message:
Rename maven modules to mirror directory structure

The naming of Maven modules in drools-applications was not
aligned with the directory structure in the git repository
of drools-applications. Therefore it was difficult to
see the strucutre of the repository in Eclipse and other
IDEs. This change amends the Maven module IDs to
reflect the repository directory structure.

This patch reset fixes the previos patch set, where many
references to maven modules internally in drools-applciations
were missed. See also changes in engine and docker repos.

Updated to reflect repo directory structure in maven
artifact groups.

Issue-ID: POLICY-238
Change-Id: I8ab1a7ecdb664045222bbbfda269135e3e449109
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@ericsson.com>

We haven't changed the Java package or class names so the .drl files should not have to change. The test cases in template.demo run the drools rules and they are all passing so I don't think we ahve to change the .drl files.

Patch Set 2:

I checked for references to drools-applications modules in the rest of policy:
find . -type f -exec grep drools-applications {} \; -print | grep '^\.' | grep -v '^\./drools-applications'
./docker/config/pe/push-policies.sh
./docker/vagrant/setup_policy.sh
./docker/pom.xml
./docker/README.md
./engine/docs/platform/installation.rst
./engine/docs/platform/swarch_pdp.rst
./engine/docs/release-notes/new-features.rst
./engine/docs/release-notes/bug-fixes.rst
./engine/ONAP-PAP-REST/target/ONAP-PAP-REST-1.2.0-SNAPSHOT/WEB-INF/lib/controlloop-common-policy_yaml-1.2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar
./engine/ONAP-PAP-REST/pom.xml
./engine/BRMSGateway/dependency.json
./engine/BRMSGateway/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/brmsInterface/BRMSPush.java
./engine/packages/base/src/files/install/servers/brmsgw/dependency.json

Having checked all the files on the list above, the only places where drools-applications sub-modules are referenced are in the files below. I'll submit a change on 'engine" to fix those:
./engine/ONAP-PAP-REST/pom.xml
./engine/BRMSGateway/dependency.json

Patch Set 4:

Thanks for the work Liam.

I was thinking instead of carrying the hierarchy in the artifact-id.  It seems to me that it could be carry it over instead in the group id, such as:

org.onap.policy.drools-applications:drools-applications   -->  drools-applications/

org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common:common    -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.templates:templates -->  drools-applications/controlloop/templates/
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.packages:packages  -->  drools-applications/controlloop/packages/

org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common.actors:actors          -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/actors/
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common:model-impl      -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/model-impl/
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common:eventmanager    -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/eventmanager/
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common:feature-controlloop-utils    -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/feature-controlloop-utils/
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common:msb    -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/msb
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common:simulators    -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/simulators

org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common.actors:appc          -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/actors/appc
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common.actors:lcm          -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/actors/lcm
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common.actors:service-provider  -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/actors/service-provider
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common.actors:so          -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/actors/so
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common.actors:test          -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/actors/test
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.common.actors:vfc          -->  drools-applications/controlloop/common/actors/vfc

org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.templates:archetype-cl-amsterdam  -->  drools-applications/controlloop/templates/archetype-cl-amsterdam
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.templates:demo  -->  drools-applications/controlloop/templates/demo

org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.packages:basex  -->  drools-applications/controlloop/packages/basex
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.packages:apps  -->  drools-applications/controlloop/packages/apps
org.onap.policy.drools-applications.controlloop.packages:artifacts  -->  drools-applications/controlloop/packages/artifacts

It is also possible to have the reactor at some levels ommitted, I think the <modules> can contain an arbitrary directory n-levels down, but I don't think is necessary here, as you have it.

There may be some side effects on scripts in this project, such as "create-cl-amsterdam" that generates a control loop app from the amsterdam archetype in labs.
The artifacts/pom.xml should package the applications too with the name changes.

Outside this, policy/engine and brmsgw may have impacts, and perhaps clamp.   policy/docker may have some changes there too.

You may have covered those changes, as I didn't go in full detail to verify, first wanted to start conversation about the naming.   

Let me know your thoughts.

Patch Set 4:

Hi Jorge,

I didn't realise we were permitted to play with the group ID. In Ericsson internal work the group IDs are fixed and we can't change them so in ONAP we have more freedom!

I agree, it would be much better to work with he group IDs rather than the artifact IDs because then the structure would be reflected in the Nexus repo as well. I'll modify the structure for that.

I didn't understand your comment about omitting the reactor at some levels; was this that we would jump down a certain level of hierarchies in the directory structure ignoring intermediate levels?

On side effects, with these sort of changes I agree that doing a full build passing unit tests is not enough. We need to do a full build of the entire policy framework, and deploy it on a production-like environment to verify that we have no problems. The build and deployment here is complex so it would be a miracle if we got all the changes perfectly correct first time. However, it's pretty easy to make the changes so after a few tries I think we'll get it right.

Patch Set 4:

Great, thanks Liam.  With regards to the skipping levels.  I understand that you could have a module for example 
<module>controlloop/common</module>, that will basically skip the need for a pom.xml in controlloop.  I don't see clear applicability, but just wanted to bring it up, in case you do perhaps.  Yes, I agree with you, we can submit what we think we have to change, and then we can test in one of our labs, to make sure all ok.  Thank you!

Patch Set 4:

OK, I get it. I have used that in the past, I agree, I don't think we need to do that here.
Uploaded patch set 5.
